text.div: intentionally left empty / not meaningful

tr.td: Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
60 ACTUAT Fluticasone propionate 0.25 MG/ACTUAT / salmeterol 0.05 MG/ACTUAT Dry Powder Inhaler
Albuterol (as albuterol sulfate) 5 mg/mL solution for inhalation
2.5 mg (0.5 mL of 5 mg/mL solution) via nebulizer every 6 hours as needed
Amlodipine (as amlodipine besylate) 2.5 mg oral tablet
1 tablet (2.5 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (40 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (20 mg) by mouth once daily
Metformin hydrochloride 500 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
1 tablet (50 mg) by mouth once daily
Galantamine (as galantamine hydrobromide) 4 mg oral tablet
Levothyroxine sodium anhydrous 75 microgram oral tablet
1 spray (400 micrograms) under the tongue as needed for chest pain, repeat every 5 minutes as needed, up to 3 sprays in 15 minutes
Metoprolol tartrate (as metoprolol succinate) 100 mg prolonged-release oral tablet
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Development of individualized plan of care

ul.li: Patient will monitor and record weight daily to detect early signs of fluid retention.
Patient will limit daily sodium intake to reduce fluid overload risk.
Patient will restrict daily fluid intake as recommended to prevent fluid overload.
Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms of heart failure exacerbation such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.
Patient will take heart failure medications as prescribed without missed doses.
Support and maintain current level of cognitive function and daily living abilities in patient with Alzheimer's disease.

tr.td: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease clinical management plan

ul.li: Patient will abstain from tobacco use to slow progression of pulmonary emphysema.
Maintain oxygen saturation at or above 92% on room air or prescribed supplemental oxygen.
Reduce acute COPD exacerbations to no more than 1 per year.
Patient will be able to walk at least 100 meters without significant dyspnea.
Patient will receive annual influenza vaccination and pneumococcal vaccination as recommended.
Maintain HbA1c below 7.5% to reduce risk of diabetes complications.
Maintain fasting blood glucose between 80 and 130 mg/dL.
Demonstrate understanding of diabetes self-care including medication, diet, and symptom monitoring.
Patient will receive education on lifestyle modifications to help control blood pressure, including diet, exercise, and medication adherence.

tr.td: Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Blood pressure panel with all children optional
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Neuropathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus

ul.li: Patient will monitor and record weight daily to detect early signs of fluid retention.
Patient will limit daily sodium intake to reduce fluid overload risk.
Patient will restrict daily fluid intake as recommended to prevent fluid overload.
Patient will recognize and promptly report symptoms of heart failure exacerbation such as increased shortness of breath, swelling, or rapid weight gain.
Patient will take heart failure medications as prescribed without missed doses.

ul.li: Patient will abstain from tobacco use to slow progression of pulmonary emphysema.
Maintain oxygen saturation at or above 92% on room air or prescribed supplemental oxygen.
Reduce acute COPD exacerbations to no more than 1 per year.
Patient will be able to walk at least 100 meters without significant dyspnea.
Patient will receive annual influenza vaccination and pneumococcal vaccination as recommended.
